name: checklist-full-v7
description: Reliance-grade audit checklist — 159 rules across 13 sections. Source-traceable to Checklist_21_6_2026. Covers automatable subset only — diagnostics accelerate review but do not replace human judgment, source-document review or commercial omission testing.
source_standard: ICAEW FMC + FAST + PwC GMG + ASIC RG170 + ASAE3450 (v7: ICAEW FMC gap-fill pass)
automation_note: Sections 10-13 are partially automatable. Rules marked manual_only:true require source documents, formula text inspection, or human judgment. Claude returns structured uncertain findings with guidance for the human reviewer.

tier1.label: No Excel formula errors anywhere in workbook
tier1.fix_instruction: Investigate and correct at source. Never replace with a hardcoded value or mask with IFERROR. Identify the root cause: broken link, missing reference, division by zero, or lookup failure.

tier1.label: Minimum sheet structure present
tier1.fix_instruction: File must contain at least 2 recognisable financial model sheets. Verify the correct file was uploaded.

tier1.label: Model Issues tab contains unresolved items
tier1.fix_instruction: Resolve all open items in the Model Issues tab before submitting for reliance review.

tier1.label: Balance sheet check sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a balance sheet check row showing Assets minus Liabilities minus Equity equals zero for every period.

tier1.label: Cash flow reconciliation sheet exists
tier1.fix_instruction: Add a cash reconciliation check row: change in balance sheet cash equals operating plus investing plus financing cash flows.

tier1.label: No negative PP&E values
tier1.fix_instruction: Gross PP&E cannot be negative. Review the asset roll-forward on the AFS sheet.

tier1.label: No uncontrolled external workbook links
tier1.fix_instruction: Remove or document all external workbook links. External links create hidden dependencies that can silently break when source files move or change.

tier1.label: No negative cash balance in any period
tier1.fix_instruction: Unexplained negative cash balance found. Any funding gap must be explicitly flagged and sized. Review funding waterfall and debt availability.

tier1.label: Actuals and forecast separated by explicit flags
tier1.fix_instruction: Actuals and forecast must be separated by explicit actual/forecast flags rather than manual overwriting of formulas.

tier1.label: Workbook opens without repair warnings or corruption
tier1.fix_instruction: Workbook shows signs of corruption or repair. Open in Excel, accept any repairs, save a clean copy, and re-upload. Investigate the source of corruption before relying on any outputs.

tier1.label: No unresolved circular references
tier1.fix_instruction: Resolve or document all circular references. Intentional circular references must be documented on the Inputs sheet and confirmed to converge reliably.

tier1.label: Actuals and forecast period flags are mutually exclusive
tier1.fix_instruction: Period flags must be mutually exclusive — no column can be both actual and forecast. Review the Timing sheet flag logic.

tier1.label: No hidden rows or columns within calculation areas
tier1.fix_instruction: Unhide all rows/columns containing formulas or supporting detail. Hidden rows are easily missed during review and easy to change or delete inadvertently. Hiding fully empty columns/rows beyond the used range for navigation purposes is acceptable and not flagged.

tier1.label: No white-font or non-displaying-format cells with visible content
tier1.fix_instruction: Cell content is being hidden via font colour matching the fill colour (or a custom number format such as ;;; that suppresses display) rather than through a proper hide/group mechanism. Reformat so the value is visible, or remove it if it is not needed.

tier1.label: Worksheet names are descriptive, not default or containing mathematical operators
tier1.fix_instruction: Rename worksheets to clear, descriptive names. Avoid default names (Sheet1, Sheet2) and avoid mathematical operators (+, -, *, /) in worksheet names, as these can be misread inside formula references.

tier1.label: No array (CSE) formulas present
tier1.fix_instruction: Array formulas (Ctrl+Shift+Enter / CSE formulas, or explicit dynamic-array functions used in an array context) are hard to audit and easy to break by inserting or deleting a single cell. Rebuild using standard row-by-row or helper-column formulas, or a modern non-array equivalent.

tier1.label: Iterative calculation and 'precision as displayed' settings are off or documented
tier1.source_section: Avoid circular references / Avoid unnecessary rounding
tier1.fix_instruction: If 'Enable iterative calculations' is on, confirm this is an intentional, documented circularity (not an accidental workaround for a circular-reference warning) and that it converges reliably. If 'Precision as displayed' is on, turn it off — it silently rewrites underlying values to match their rounded display and can mask real balances or corrupt historical actuals.

tier1.label: Data validation or input restrictions applied to constrained inputs
tier1.fix_instruction: No data validation rules were found anywhere in the workbook despite dedicated input sheets. Where inputs are constrained or interdependent (e.g. an allocation table that must sum to 100%, a rate that must stay within a plausible range), add data validation with a clear, specific error message — not a generic one — or compute the final entry in an allocation from the others rather than allowing it to be typed.

tier2.label: Model purpose and audience documented
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a cover sheet or README documenting model purpose, audience, reliance level, and key limitations.

tier2.label: FAST principles applied — no opaque constructs
tier2.fix_instruction: Simplify flagged formula. Break into intermediate steps. Remove opaque constructs.

tier2.label: Inputs centralised — no business assumptions hard-coded in calculation sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move hard-coded business assumptions to the Inputs sheet and replace with cell references.

tier2.label: No duplicated calculation logic across sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Remove duplicate calculation blocks. Each calculation should exist once and be referenced elsewhere.

tier2.label: No mixed monthly and quarterly logic on same sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: Annual views must be explicit aggregations only. No independent annual logic that can diverge from the periodic engine.

tier2.label: Every key output traceable to live formulas — no pasted values
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace pasted values on dashboard and output sheets with live cell references.

tier2.label: Every key assumption has reasonable support
tier2.fix_instruction: Document the basis for each material assumption. Assumptions must not be merely management targets or balancing numbers.

tier2.label: No critical calculations on output sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Move calculation logic to a supporting schedule sheet and replace with a reference link on the output sheet.

tier2.label: Single master timeline drives all time-series sheets
tier2.fix_instruction: Link all sheet timelines to the master timeline on the Inputs or Timing sheet.

tier2.label: No hidden sheets affecting results
tier2.fix_instruction: Unhide all sheets and confirm none contain material calculations affecting outputs without disclosure.

tier2.label: Model extendable without formula rewrites
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm that adding extra periods or facilities can be done by copying existing blocks without rewriting logic.

tier2.label: No volatile functions in core model engine
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace OFFSET and INDIRECT functions in core calculation areas with static references.

tier2.label: Logic flows left-to-right, top-to-bottom — no backward dependencies
tier2.fix_instruction: Restructure sheets so logic flows left-to-right across tabs and top-to-bottom within tabs.

tier2.label: Live model map exists showing every sheet and its purpose
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a model map tab or cover sheet section listing every worksheet, its purpose, and criticality.

tier2.label: Colour conventions distinguish inputs, formulas, outputs
tier2.fix_instruction: Apply consistent colour conventions: blue/yellow for inputs, white for formulas, green/grey for outputs.

tier2.label: Opening forecast balance sheet equals latest actual closing balance sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: The opening forecast balance sheet must equal the latest actual closing balance sheet after all reclassifications.

tier2.label: Period flags are mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive
tier2.fix_instruction: Period flags must be mutually exclusive and reconcile to total period count. No period should be uncategorised or double-counted.

tier2.label: All inputs on dedicated sheets with labels, units, and source
tier2.fix_instruction: Consolidate all inputs to the Inputs sheet. Add labels, units, sign conventions, source references, and last update date.

tier2.label: No business assumptions hard-coded in formula areas
tier2.fix_instruction: Move all business assumptions to the Inputs sheet and replace formula constants with cell references.

tier2.label: Historical data reconciles to source documents
tier2.fix_instruction: Add reconciliation tables showing historical financial statements tie to source documents.

tier2.label: Historical data is current and not stale
tier2.fix_instruction: Update historical data to the most recent available period.

tier2.label: Key assumptions entered once and referenced consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise all key assumptions on the Inputs sheet and replace all instances elsewhere with a single cell reference.

tier2.label: Every input has at least one live dependent
tier2.fix_instruction: Identify and remove or quarantine inputs with no live dependents.

tier2.label: Annual assumptions correctly converted to monthly or quarterly
tier2.fix_instruction: Verify annual assumptions are converted to monthly or quarterly using correct compounding or pro-rating logic.

tier2.label: Nominal and real assumptions not mixed without conversion
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm all assumptions are consistently nominal or real. If mixing, add an explicit inflation conversion step.

tier2.label: GST-inclusive and exclusive amounts not mixed in same calculation stream
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm the model is consistently GST-inclusive or GST-exclusive throughout.

tier2.label: Scenario-controlled inputs linked to scenario table — not independently set
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise scenario selection to a single input cell. Link all scenario-dependent assumptions through INDEX or IF logic.

tier2.label: Single formula per row on all time-series
tier2.fix_instruction: Standardise the formula across each flagged row. Check for mid-row edits or anchoring inconsistencies.

tier2.label: Consistent sign convention throughout workbook
tier2.fix_instruction: Review and standardise sign conventions. Document the chosen convention on the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: No unintended circular references
tier2.fix_instruction: Document any intentional circular references. Confirm they converge reliably and can be switched off for audit.

tier2.label: Unit consistency and conversion checks
tier2.fix_instruction: Check for unit conversion errors. Add explicit unit labels to all input rows.

tier2.label: Lookup function integrity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH and XLOOKUP functions. Confirm exact-match settings and range references.

tier2.label: IFERROR and error suppression does not hide real calculation defects
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all IFERROR, IFNA, and ISERROR usage. Confirm each is suppressing an expected condition, not hiding a real defect.

tier2.label: SUM and SUMIFS ranges cover full time-series without gaps or double counts
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all SUM/SUMIFS ranges for partial range errors, subtotal double-counts, and omitted inserted rows.

tier2.label: IRR and NPV formulas use correct timing, sign convention, and dates
tier2.fix_instruction: Review every IRR/XIRR/NPV/XNPV formula for correct timing convention, sign convention, and terminal value inclusion.

tier2.label: MIN and MAX constraints reflect commercial terms — not masking problems
tier2.fix_instruction: Review every MIN/MAX constraint and confirm the cap or floor reflects documented commercial terms.

tier2.label: Balance sheet balances for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Balance sheet does not balance. Review equity roll-forward, retained earnings, and debt closing balances.

tier2.label: Change in cash equals net cash flow for every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Cash flow does not reconcile to balance sheet. Review all three cash flow sections for missing items.

tier2.label: Net income flows through to retained earnings
tier2.fix_instruction: Retained earnings roll-forward does not reconcile. Review the link from the IFS net profit line to retained earnings.

tier2.label: Equity movements reconcile between balance sheet and cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Equity movement appears in balance sheet but not in financing cash flow or vice versa.

tier2.label: Working capital movements reconcile between balance sheet and operating cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Working capital movement in balance sheet does not match operating cash flow.

tier2.label: No logically impossible balance sheet positions
tier2.fix_instruction: Logically impossible balance sheet value found. Review the flagged line item.

tier2.label: Income tax calculation is reconcilable
tier2.fix_instruction: Tax calculation appears disconnected from taxable profits. Review the tax schedule.

tier2.label: Deferred tax treatment disclosed
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a deferred tax schedule or document the simplification explicitly.

tier2.label: No P&L, balance sheet or cash flow line is a plug
tier2.fix_instruction: Identify and remove all plugs. Understand and fix the underlying cause of the imbalance.

tier2.label: Annual statements reconcile to monthly or quarterly detail
tier2.fix_instruction: Annual totals must not sum balance-sheet accounts across periods — only flow items should be summed.

tier2.label: Fixed assets roll from opening NBV through additions, disposals and depreciation
tier2.fix_instruction: Fixed asset roll-forward must close: opening NBV + additions - disposals - depreciation = closing NBV.

tier2.label: Debt roll-forward identity holds for each facility
tier2.fix_instruction: Debt roll-forward does not balance. Review drawdown, repayment, capitalised interest, and fee amortisation.

tier2.label: Interest calculated from referenced rates — no hard-coded rates
tier2.fix_instruction: Replace any hard-coded interest rates in the Debt sheet with references to the Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Covenant calculations follow documented definitions
tier2.fix_instruction: Review covenant calculation definitions against the term sheet.

tier2.label: Capex phasing feeds into PP&E, balance sheet and investing cash flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the Capex schedule links to both the AFS balance sheet and the Cons investing activities section.

tier2.label: Cash waterfall sequence is valid
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the cash waterfall sequence: revenue → O&M → taxes → debt service → reserves → distributions.

tier2.label: Cash sweep and lock-up mechanisms operate correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cash sweep and lock-up logic. Under covenant breach, distributions should be blocked.

tier2.label: DSRA and reserve accounts reconcile correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Review DSRA and other reserve accounts. Confirm they correctly size, fund, release, and replenish.

tier2.label: Ownership percentages sum to 100% in every period
tier2.fix_instruction: Ownership percentages must sum to exactly 100% in every period.

tier2.label: Preferred return accrues at correct rate with correct compounding
tier2.fix_instruction: Verify preferred return accrues on the correct base, at the correct rate, with correct compounding.

tier2.label: Distributions cannot exceed available cash after all senior obligations
tier2.fix_instruction: Distributions must not exceed available cash after tax, reserves, and senior debt service.

tier2.label: Capitalised, cash and accrued interest separated — not double counted
tier2.fix_instruction: Check capitalised interest, cash interest, and accrued interest are separately modelled without double counting.

tier2.label: Revenue equals price times volume consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Add explicit price times volume calculations on the Ops sheet referencing price inputs from Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Production volumes within installed capacity
tier2.fix_instruction: Review production volume assumptions. Volumes must not exceed modelled capacity.

tier2.label: Fixed versus variable cost classification is sensible
tier2.fix_instruction: Review cost classification. Variable costs should move with volume in a plausible way.

tier2.label: Margins plausible relative to history and industry norms
tier2.fix_instruction: Review margin assumptions on the Inputs sheet. Compare against historical periods and industry benchmarks.

tier2.label: IRR, NPV and valuation metrics within plausible range
tier2.fix_instruction: Review discount rate assumptions and cash flow profile.

tier2.label: Revenue recognition separated from cash receipts, deposits, and settlements
tier2.fix_instruction: Revenue recognition timing must be separated from cash receipt timing.

tier2.label: Bad debts, rebates, vacancy, churn and ramp-up included where relevant
tier2.fix_instruction: Check that bad debts, rebates, vacancy, churn, and ramp-up adjustments are included where commercially relevant.

tier2.label: Payroll uses headcount, salary, super, payroll tax and annual escalation
tier2.fix_instruction: Payroll must be built from headcount times salary, including superannuation, payroll tax, and annual escalation.

tier2.label: Capex and maintenance capex separated from operating costs
tier2.fix_instruction: Capex and maintenance capex must be separated from operating costs.

tier2.label: Scenario engine centrally controlled
tier2.fix_instruction: Centralise scenario selection to a single input cell on the Inputs or Dashboard sheet.

tier2.label: Core sensitivities produce directionally correct responses
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the link between the sensitivity input and the affected output.

tier2.label: Zero revenue stress test passes without model errors
tier2.fix_instruction: Setting main revenue drivers to zero should produce zero revenue and a structurally intact model.

tier2.label: Date and period edge cases do not break model
tier2.fix_instruction: Test partial periods and period boundary shifts.

tier2.label: Downside cases do not suppress funding gaps or covenant breaches
tier2.fix_instruction: Funding gaps, covenant breaches, and distribution locks must surface explicitly in downside scenarios.

tier2.label: Sensitivity outputs reconcile to base when sensitivity variable equals base assumption
tier2.fix_instruction: When a sensitivity variable equals the base assumption, the sensitivity output must reconcile to the base model output.

tier2.label: No debt available stress test — equity funding gap logic works correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Setting debt availability to zero should produce a visible equity or funding gap.

tier2.label: Delayed start date test — timing shifts costs, interest and revenue correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: Delaying the project start date should shift operating costs, capex, capitalised interest, and revenue in the same direction.

tier2.label: Dedicated checks sheet aggregates all critical tests
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a dedicated Checks sheet aggregating all critical model integrity tests with clear PASS or FAIL flags.

tier2.label: Change log exists with version history
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a change log tab recording date, editor, description of changes, and version tag for each material edit.

tier2.label: Independent review evidence recorded
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a note indicating that a peer or independent review has been conducted, with date and scope noted.

tier2.label: Valuation and return metrics calculated from live cash flows — not pasted values
tier2.fix_instruction: IRR, NPV, equity multiple, and all return metrics must be calculated directly from live investor or project cash flows.

tier2.label: Accounting basis documented — accrual vs cash treatment and reporting framework
tier2.fix_instruction: Document the accounting basis (accrual or cash) and reporting framework (IFRS, AASB, management accounts) on the cover sheet or Inputs sheet.

tier2.label: Opening balances reconcile to latest audited or actual source accounts
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a reconciliation table showing opening balance sheet balances tie to the latest audited financial statements or management accounts.

tier2.label: Chart of accounts mapping complete — every account maps to exactly one statement line
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a chart of accounts mapping tab showing every GL account mapped to exactly one P&L, balance sheet, or cash flow line. No account should be unmapped or double-mapped.

tier2.label: Each balance sheet line rolls forward: opening + movements = closing
tier2.fix_instruction: Every balance sheet account must roll forward: opening balance + additions - reductions +/- reclassifications = closing balance. Add explicit roll-forward schedules for all material accounts.

tier2.label: Opening balance for each period equals prior period closing balance
tier2.fix_instruction: Opening balance must equal prior period closing balance for every balance sheet account in every period. Any discontinuity is a critical error requiring explanation.

tier2.label: Net profit after tax flows through retained earnings and agrees to equity roll-forward
tier2.fix_instruction: NPAT must flow directly to retained earnings. The equity roll-forward must reconcile: opening equity + NPAT + equity contributions - distributions = closing equity.

tier2.label: Cash flow statement derived from P&L and balance sheet movements — not independently hard-coded
tier2.fix_instruction: The cash flow statement must be derived from P&L line items and balance sheet movements. Independently hardcoded cash flows are a fatal error — they can mask errors in both the P&L and balance sheet.

tier2.label: Closing cash calculated once from opening cash plus net cash movement
tier2.fix_instruction: Closing cash must be calculated as: opening cash + operating cash flow + investing cash flow + financing cash flow. It must not be independently derived or hard-coded.

tier2.label: Working capital separately models receivables, payables, accruals and prepayments
tier2.fix_instruction: Working capital must separately model trade receivables, trade payables, accruals, prepayments, and deposits using explicit debtor days, creditor days, and timing assumptions.

tier2.label: Revenue recognition separated from invoicing, deposits, deferred revenue and cash receipts
tier2.fix_instruction: Revenue must be recognised when earned, not when invoiced or received. Separate deferred revenue, contract liabilities, and customer deposits from earned revenue on the balance sheet.

tier2.label: Costs recognised on accrual basis with payment timing captured separately
tier2.fix_instruction: Costs must be recognised when incurred, not when paid. Accrued expenses and prepaid costs must appear on the balance sheet. Payment timing must be captured separately in the cash flow.

tier2.label: Capex, development inventory and WIP classified correctly and transferred at correct trigger
tier2.fix_instruction: Capex must flow to PP&E or WIP on the balance sheet and to investing activities in the cash flow. WIP must transfer to completed assets at the correct trigger point (e.g. practical completion, commissioning).

tier2.label: Depreciation and amortisation schedules tie to asset balances and P&L charges
tier2.fix_instruction: D&A on the P&L must equal the sum of depreciation charges from the PP&E and intangible asset roll-forward schedules. Any difference requires explanation.

tier2.label: Tax expense, current tax payable, deferred tax and cash tax paid reconcile
tier2.fix_instruction: Tax must reconcile fully: P&L tax expense = current tax + deferred tax movement. Current tax payable roll-forward: opening + current charge - cash tax paid = closing. Cash tax paid must flow through operating activities.

tier2.label: GST/VAT, withholding tax and payroll tax balances roll forward and settle correctly
tier2.fix_instruction: GST/VAT, withholding tax, and payroll tax must roll forward on the balance sheet and settle through cash flows at the correct frequency and timing.

tier2.label: Debt fees, capitalised interest, accrued interest and cash interest classified consistently
tier2.fix_instruction: Debt arrangement fees must be amortised through P&L or capitalised to the debt balance. Capitalised interest during construction must not appear in P&L. Accrued interest must appear on the balance sheet and settle through financing cash flows.

tier2.label: Equity contributions, shareholder loans, dividends and distributions flow through correct accounts
tier2.fix_instruction: Equity contributions must flow to paid-up capital on the balance sheet and to financing activities in the cash flow. Shareholder loans must be classified as debt or equity consistently. Dividends and distributions must reduce retained earnings and appear in financing activities.

tier2.label: No accounting line item used as balancing plug
tier2.fix_instruction: No line item should exist solely to force a reconciliation. Every line must represent a genuine economic item. Identify and eliminate all plugs and understand the root cause of any imbalance.

tier2.label: Balance sheet balances within stated tolerance and tolerance is immaterial
tier2.fix_instruction: If a balance sheet tolerance exists, confirm it is less than 0.01% of total assets or an immaterial dollar amount. Tolerances above this threshold indicate a material reconciling error that must be identified and fixed.

tier2.label: Sign convention follows explicit rule and is consistent across all statements
tier2.fix_instruction: Document the sign convention on the Inputs or cover sheet. Costs, outflows, and liabilities must follow a consistent sign rule across P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow. Mixed conventions are a common source of reconciliation errors.

tier2.label: Accounting classifications distinguish capitalised costs, expensed costs, financing and operating flows
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm every cost item is correctly classified: capitalised (→ PP&E, investing), expensed (→ P&L, operating), or financing (→ debt/equity, financing activities). Misclassification distorts all three statements simultaneously.

tier2.label: Audit checks exist for retained earnings, cash, debt, tax, working capital and fixed asset roll-forwards
tier2.fix_instruction: Add dedicated check rows for every material roll-forward: retained earnings, cash, debt, tax payable, working capital, and PP&E. Each check must show zero or within tolerance in every period.

tier2.label: Actuals cut-over date stated clearly and all periods before it locked to actual data
tier2.fix_instruction: Add the actuals cut-over date to the Inputs or Timing sheet. All periods before the cut-over must be locked to actual data and must not update when forecast assumptions change.

tier2.label: Actual and forecast flags mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive, and drive all time-sensitive formulas
tier2.fix_instruction: Every period must be flagged as either actual or forecast — not both and not neither. The flag must drive all time-sensitive formulas so the model automatically updates when the cut-over date changes.

tier2.label: Actual results reconcile to trial balance, GL export, bank statements or management accounts
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a reconciliation tab showing actual P&L and balance sheet figures tie to the source system (trial balance, general ledger export, or management accounts) for each historical period.

tier2.label: Manual actuals adjustments separately listed with owner, date, source and rationale
tier2.fix_instruction: List all manual adjustments to actual data in a dedicated adjustments register with: the line item adjusted, the amount, the owner, the date, the source document reference, and the rationale.

tier2.label: Forecast formulas begin immediately after actuals cut-over and do not overwrite actual periods
tier2.fix_instruction: Forecast formulas must begin exactly one period after the actuals cut-over. Forecast logic must not apply to actual periods. Check that the actual/forecast flag correctly switches between data sources.

tier2.label: Opening forecast balance sheet equals latest actual closing balance sheet with no unexplained reset
tier2.fix_instruction: The first forecast period opening balance sheet must equal the last actual period closing balance sheet exactly. Any difference must be explained and documented as a specific adjustment.

tier2.label: Imported data ranges complete, de-duplicated and free from blank or duplicate keys
tier2.fix_instruction: Review all imported data ranges. Confirm no blank key rows, no duplicate key records, no missing periods, and no stale import dates.

tier2.label: Source documents, data extract dates and version references recorded for all historical data
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a source reference table showing: data type, source system, extract date, version, and the person who imported it. This is essential for audit trail.

tier2.label: Model covers every material revenue stream, cost, capex, tax, funding source and distribution flow
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the model scope against the commercial structure. Identify any material revenue streams, cost categories, tax items, funding sources, or distribution flows that are absent without explanation.

tier2.label: Lifecycle phases complete — acquisition, construction, operations, refinance, exit where relevant
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm all relevant lifecycle phases are modelled: acquisition/commencement, construction/ramp-up, stabilised operations, refinance events, exit, and wind-down. Missing phases must be explicitly excluded with rationale.

tier2.label: Timing assumptions capture lead times, delays, payment terms and commissioning periods
tier2.fix_instruction: Review timing assumptions for: construction lead times, approval delays, payment terms, settlement timing, commissioning periods, and ramp-up curves. Missing timing assumptions lead to overstated early cash flows.

tier2.label: Contractual terms from leases, loans, sale contracts and shareholder documents translated into model logic
tier2.fix_instruction: Cross-reference key contractual terms against model logic: lease terms, loan covenants, sale contract conditions, shareholder agreement provisions. Any material term not reflected in the model must be documented as an exclusion.

tier2.label: Non-recurring costs, transaction costs, legal fees, stamp duty and setup costs included or explicitly excluded
tier2.fix_instruction: Check whether transaction costs, legal fees, adviser fees, stamp duty, and setup costs are included in the model. If excluded, document the exclusion and confirm the materiality.

tier2.label: Maintenance capex, replacement capex and lifecycle capex not omitted from long-term cases
tier2.fix_instruction: Long-term models must include maintenance capex, asset replacement capex, and lifecycle capex. Omitting these overstates free cash flow in later periods and understates the funding requirement.

tier2.label: Contingencies modelled against correct cost base and can be stress-tested independently
tier2.fix_instruction: Contingencies must be applied as a percentage of the correct cost base (typically hard construction costs) and must be separately identifiable so they can be removed or increased in stress tests.

tier2.label: Insurance, rates, utilities, compliance costs and recurring overheads captured with escalation
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm insurance premiums, council rates, utilities, compliance costs, and other recurring overheads are included in the operating cost build with appropriate escalation assumptions.

tier2.label: Off-model adjustments and judgemental overlays listed, quantified and reconciled to outputs
tier2.fix_instruction: All off-model adjustments must be listed in a register with: the item, amount, owner, basis, and a reconciliation showing how the adjustment flows to the reported output.

tier2.label: Challenger or reasonableness check exists for key outputs — revenue, EBITDA, funding need, valuation, IRR
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a reasonableness check section showing an independent cross-check of key outputs using a different method (e.g. comparable transactions, rule of thumb, simplified DCF). This confirms outputs are in a plausible range.

tier2.label: Version control records file name, date, author, reviewer, purpose and change summary
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a version control register on the cover sheet recording: file name, version number, date, author, reviewer, purpose, and a summary of changes since the prior version.

tier2.label: Change log identifies material formula, assumption, structure and source-data changes
tier2.fix_instruction: The change log must identify every material change: formula logic, assumption values, structural changes, and source data updates. Generic entries like 'updated model' are insufficient.

tier2.label: Workbook calculation mode, iterative settings and precision settings documented and appropriate
tier2.fix_instruction: Document the Excel calculation mode (automatic/manual), iterative calculation settings, and precision settings on the cover sheet. Manual calculation mode must be disclosed. Iterative calculation must be documented if used for circular references.

tier2.label: Macros, VBA, scripts, add-ins and external data connections documented, reviewed and safe to run
tier2.fix_instruction: List all macros, VBA scripts, add-ins, and external data connections on the cover sheet. Each must be reviewed for accuracy, security, and portability. The model must not depend on add-ins unavailable to the reviewer.

tier2.label: Protected, hidden and locked areas do not prevent review of material logic
tier2.fix_instruction: Remove or document all sheet and workbook protection. No material calculation should be hidden or locked in a way that prevents the reviewer from reading the formula logic.

tier2.label: Named ranges, tables and links are current, used and not pointing to deleted or legacy ranges
tier2.fix_instruction: Review the Name Manager for broken, unused, or legacy named ranges. Remove or update all stale names. Broken named ranges can cause formula errors that are invisible in normal cell view.

tier2.label: Instructions tab explains model purpose, scenario controls, refresh steps, output interpretation and limitations
tier2.fix_instruction: Add or complete an Instructions tab covering: model purpose, how to change scenarios, how to refresh data, how to interpret outputs, known limitations, and who to contact for questions.

tier2.label: Outputs intended for lenders, investors or management tied to audited model — not manually overwritten
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm that all outputs provided to external parties (lenders, investors, board) are live references to model calculations and have not been manually overwritten. Pasted or overwritten outputs are a serious integrity failure.

tier2.label: Review status clear for every material issue — owner, severity, remediation and sign-off
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a model issues register on the cover sheet or a dedicated Issues tab showing: issue description, severity, owner, remediation action, target date, and sign-off status for every open item.

tier2.label: Model fit for handover — no personal paths, broken links, missing source files or unexplained warnings
tier2.fix_instruction: Check for personal file paths, broken external links, missing source files, unsupported add-ins, and unexplained warnings. The model must open and run cleanly on any machine without the original author present.

tier2.label: Revenue on consolidated statements is linked to production volumes, not calculated independently
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #1 (revenue not linked to Ops)
tier2.fix_instruction: Link the revenue calculation on the consolidated/financial statement sheet directly to the production volume and price assumptions on the operations sheet, so a change to either flows through automatically.

tier2.label: Capex reduces to a sustaining-only level after commissioning, rather than continuing at development-phase levels
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #2 (capex after commissioning)
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm capex drops to a lower, clearly-labelled sustaining-capex level once the project moves from development/construction into steady-state operations, rather than continuing at development-phase magnitude.

tier2.label: Depreciation reduces taxable income (tax shield modelled), rather than tax being calculated independently of depreciation
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #3 (tax shield not modelled)
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm the tax expense calculation deducts depreciation/amortisation from taxable income before applying the tax rate, rather than being calculated from a pre-depreciation profit figure.

tier2.label: Debt repayments are sculpted to a target DSCR/cover ratio, or the use of equal instalments instead is explicitly justified
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #4 (debt sculpting not applied)
tier2.fix_instruction: If debt repayments are equal instalments rather than sculpted to a target DSCR, confirm this is a deliberate, disclosed choice rather than an oversight — equal instalments are not automatically wrong, but understate early-period equity returns relative to a sculpted profile and should be a stated assumption, not a default.

tier2.label: A mine rehabilitation/closure cost provision exists and accrues over the mine life
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #5 (closure costs missing)
tier2.fix_instruction: Add a rehabilitation/closure cost provision that accrues over the mine life and is reflected in cash flow and the balance sheet, rather than being omitted or appearing only as an unfunded lump sum in the final year.

tier2.label: Royalties are classified as an operating cost, not embedded in the tax line
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #6 (royalties in wrong line)
tier2.fix_instruction: Move royalty expense to operating costs (above EBITDA) if it is currently combined with the income tax line — royalties and income tax are calculated on different bases and distort both EBITDA and the effective tax rate if merged.

tier2.label: FX conversion is applied consistently to all USD/AUD (or equivalent) line items, not selectively
tier2.source_section: Common mining model failure patterns #7 (FX applied inconsistently)
tier2.fix_instruction: Confirm every revenue and cost line stated in a foreign currency has FX conversion applied via the same referenced FX rate — a mix of converted and unconverted lines in the same currency-mixed model materially distorts EBITDA and downstream metrics.

tier2.label: Each input value has a single unique location — no duplicate hardcoded entry of the same assumption
tier2.fix_instruction: The same input value appears to be entered independently in more than one location rather than referenced from a single source cell. Consolidate to one input location and have all other uses reference it directly or through a documented chain, so updating the assumption in one place updates it everywhere.

tier2.label: Range names are clear, meaningful and differentiated — not cryptic abbreviations
tier2.source_section: Use clear range names where appropriate
tier2.fix_instruction: One or more named ranges use cryptic, ambiguous, or easily-confused names (e.g. 'SNR1' instead of 'SnrIntRate1'). Rename to something a reviewer unfamiliar with the model could interpret without cross-referencing a separate list.

tier2.label: Master check result is surfaced wherever the user is working, not only on a separate checks sheet
tier2.fix_instruction: A checks/audit sheet exists and aggregates tests, but its overall pass/fail status is not visible from other worksheets (e.g. via a frozen-pane summary cell or per-sheet check reference). Surface the master check result on every working sheet so a breach is noticed immediately regardless of where the user is navigating.

tier2.label: Rounding applied only at presentation layer — no mid-calculation rounding or precision-as-displayed used to force a balance
tier2.fix_instruction: One or more calculation cells apply ROUND (or equivalent) to intermediate values rather than only at final presentation, or a 'balancing' calculation appears to nudge unrounded figures to a rounded target. Remove mid-calculation rounding — carry full precision through calculations and round only in number formatting or clearly-labelled final output cells.
tier2.mode_a_note: This test cannot be reliably completed in Mode A (cell values only). Return uncertain with confidence capped at 30 and state that formula text inspection is required, unless the existing 'Rounding'-labelled plug-row heuristic (no_accounting_plugs) already surfaced a related finding.

tier2.label: Cross-sheet references are routed through local call-up cells, not buried directly inside deep calculation formulas
tier2.fix_instruction: Calculation formulas reference cells on other sheets directly, several layers deep, rather than importing the value once via a single local 'call-up' cell on the calculating sheet. Add a local reference cell for each externally-sourced value and have downstream formulas reference that local cell, so formulas can be read without a sheet qualifier and without navigating away.
tier2.mode_a_note: This test cannot be reliably completed in Mode A (cell values only). Return uncertain with confidence capped at 30 and state that formula text inspection is required.
